Patch Set 3:

(6 comments)

I wonder why all the 2.8.0 hardcoding instead of using LUSTRE_VERSION_STRING in lnet and libcfs?
Even if not directly accessible, we probably need to make something similar so that we don't go around bumping that version every time (or, which is more likely, totally forgetting about it and so it getting out of whack again really fast).

Also a patch like this would be great for the upstream client too.

Patch Set 3:

Could this be done in another patch or do you want this updated again?

Patch Set 3:

I think it's better to update this patch as the description of beautification certainly fits here.

Thanks.

Patch Set 3:

Okay. Would you be okay with odd numbers less than v1.0 since libcfs/LNet are not in sync with upstream. When both libcfs/LNet are sync with upstream we can boost it to 1.0. LNet LND need special numbering in it own right.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

commit.subject: LU-6204 build: clean up kernel module metadata
commit.message:
LU-6204 build: clean up kernel module metadata

Update static MODULE_VERSION() lines - this should be automated.

Improve MODULE_DESCRIPTION() descriptions.

Make the name of the module_init()/_exit() functions consistently
{module_name}_init and {module_name}_exit.

Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Change-Id: I1c3fe5698c7f41d971a38225650597c913500c1e
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16787
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Frank Zago <fzago@cray.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
